alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Maximum levels (MLs). Codex establishes maximum levels (MLs) for contaminants in food or feed commodities. These MLs represent the maximum concentration legally permitted to protect human health, based on sound scientific evidence, and are kept as low as possible.

alerts.title: Added: India Labelling Requirement - Allergen declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration of allergenic ingredients as per FSSAI regulations. Food products, including seasoning and snack products, containing specified allergenic ingredients must declare them on the label. Verification against the Food Safety and Standards (Labelling and Display) Regulations is required for the complete list of allergens and specific declaration rules.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'. Detailed specifications and standards for food additives are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Agricultural Chemical Residues
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against the Positive List System. Japan implements a Positive List System for agricultural chemical residues in food, requiring verification of specific limits.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Contaminant Limits - Contaminants in Food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'. Detailed specifications and standards for contaminants in food are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

alerts.title: Added: Japan Microbiology Limits - Microbiological Standards for Food
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required against 'Specifications and Standards for Food, Food Additives, etc. Under The Food Sanitation Act'. Detailed specifications and standards for microbiological limits in food are established under the Food Sanitation Act.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East Labelling Requirement - General labelling requirements for prepackaged foodstuffs
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies the general requirements for labelling of prepackaged foodstuffs. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East Labelling Requirement - General requirements for Halal food and declaration
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation sets general requirements for food products to be considered Halal, impacting ingredients, processing, and labelling for Halal claims. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East Labelling Requirement - General requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation outlines general requirements for unprocessed genetically modified agricultural products, which may necessitate specific labelling or ingredient restrictions for processed foods containing them. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Middle East Labelling Requirement - Mandatory shelf life periods for food products
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required. This technical regulation specifies mandatory shelf life periods for food products. Specific details must be verified in the full standard.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Additive Limits - Non-permitted Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Do not import or sell. An application can be submitted to SFA to evaluate the use of the additive.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants (incidental constituents)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be within SFA’s regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Contaminants exceeding regulatory limits
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Do not import or sell.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Diethylstibestrol, Hexoestrol, Dienoestrol
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited. As defined under Regulation 29(3) of the Food Regulations, veterinary drugs include antibiotic and oestrogen.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Contaminant Limits - Incidental Constituents (Contaminants) and Food Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: All food businesses must comply with regulatory limits.

alerts.title: Added: Singapore Labelling Requirement - Ingredients and Additives
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Must be declared in list of ingredients on food labels. Applies only to prepacked foods for sale in Singapore.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Crustacean shellfish, mango, peanuts, sesame seeds, milk and goat milk, eggs, tree nuts, gluten-containing cereals, soybeans, fish
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Mandatory declaration if present as ingredient or food additive. These allergens require mandatory labeling when present as an ingredient or food additive in prepackaged foods.

alerts.title: Added: Taiwan Allergen List - Sulphite (as residue sulphur dioxide)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 10. Mandatory labeling for sulphite if the residue sulphur dioxide is 10 mg/kg or more in prepackaged foods.

alerts.title: Added: Thailand Labelling Requirement - Prohibited Foods
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from production, import, or sale. Verification required to identify specific prohibited foods.. A list of foods prohibited from production, import, or sale exists and must be consulted for compliance.

alerts.title: Added: United States Additive Limits - Prohibited substances
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Prohibited from use. Prohibited substances are listed in FDA regulations (21 CFR Part 189).

alerts.title: Added: United States Allergen List - Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. The nine major food allergens are milk, eggs, fish, Crustacean shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, and sesame. This list applies to most packaged food products.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. This is a general action level for total aflatoxins in foods.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. Specific action level for peanuts and peanut products.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Aflatoxins, total
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: 20. Specific action level for pistachio nuts.

alerts.title: Added: United States Contaminant Limits - Various chemical contaminants (tolerances, action levels, guidance levels, etc.)
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: Verification required via Chemical Contaminants Transparency Tool and referenced guidance documents/regulations (21 CFR parts 109 and 509). Users must refer to the tool and linked documents for detailed information on commodity definition and application of contaminant levels.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - 'Gluten-free' claim definition
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. FDA issued a final rule defining 'gluten-free' for food labeling, including compliance requirements for fermented and hydrolyzed foods (effective 08/12/2020).

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Allergen preventive controls and accurate labeling
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. Facilities must have written procedures to control allergen cross-contact and ensure accurate allergen labeling, as per the 'Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food' rule (21 CFR part 117).

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of cochineal extract and carmine
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. Identified as allergenic substances derived from insects.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of Major Food Allergens
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. The food source of a major food allergen must appear either in parentheses following the ingredient name or immediately after/next to the ingredient list in a 'contains' statement. Specific types of tree nuts, fish species, and Crustacean shellfish must be declared.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Declaration of sulfiting agents
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: ≥10. Required when present in food at or above 10 ppm total sulfites, due to adverse reactions in sensitive individuals.

alerts.title: Added: United States Labelling Requirement - Identification of FD&C Yellow No. 5
alerts.actionRequired: Review impacted seasoning/snack formulations, labels, COAs and export approvals.
alerts.details: New: N/A. Required due to potential hypersensitivity reactions in some individuals.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. The Codex General Standard for Food Additives (GSFA, Codex STAN 192-1995) sets conditions for additive use. Specific provisions for food additives, searchable by food additive, functional class, and food category, are available in the online database. Users are encouraged to consult the Preamble for interpretation.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Additive Limits - Permitted food additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via GSFA Online Database. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: New: Verification required via Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) and General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). The Codex Committee on Contaminants in Food (CCCF) establishes and endorses permitted maximum levels or guideline levels for contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants. Specific limits must be verified by consulting the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995) and other related Codex texts.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and naturally occurring toxicants
alerts.details: Old: Maximum levels (MLs) must be legally permitted concentrations. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Contaminants and Toxins
alerts.details: Old: Verification required via General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995). Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Removed: Codex Contaminant Limits - Mycotoxins
alerts.details: Old: Prevention and reduction measures required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Additive Limits - Food additives
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Food additives approved for use in the EU and their conditions of use must be verified against the Union list of food additives, which is in Annex II of Regulation (EC) No 1333/2008.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Additive Limits - Food Additives
alerts.details: Old: Verification required. Rule no longer appeared in the latest extraction. Verify whether source changed or extraction missed it.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Commission Regulation (EC) No 2073/2005 lays down food safety criteria for relevant foodborne bacteria, their toxins and metabolites in specific foods, defining product acceptability and process hygiene.

alerts.title: Added: European Union Microbiology Limits - Salmonella prevalence
alerts.details: New: Verification required. Special guarantees regarding Salmonella prevalence apply to trade of certain live animals and products from Sweden, Finland, and Norway, and can be obtained by other EU countries with equivalent control programs. These guarantees are laid down in various Council Decisions and Regulations.

alerts.title: Removed: European Union Microbiology Limits - Foodborne bacteria, toxins, and metabolites (e.g., Salmonella, Listeria monocytogenes, Enterobacter sakazakii, staphylococcal enterotoxins, histamine)

Manufacturers must verify compliance.
